_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: San Antonio (TX) City Council batch — Districts 3, 6, 7, 9, 10. Evidence-based finalization. Roster verified current June 2026 (all five seated after the May/June 2025 municipal election). LOCAL tier (30 scoreable cells). Scores reset to null where no evidence; FALSE only where a documented vote/statement/endorsement exists; TRUE where documented alignment. SA council is officially nonpartisan — partisan lean verified per official, never inferred. Fiscal framing noted where a conservative action (e.g. opposing rainbow-sidewalk spending) was argued on budget rather than moral grounds.

profile.religion: No public affiliation found (attended a Catholic high school; not a stated personal faith)
profile.background: Commercial-litigation attorney (Whyte PLLC); J.D. with honors, St. Mary's University (2006); business degree, Wake Forest. First elected D10 in 2023, re-elected May 2025 (69%). The council's most prominent conservative (with Misty Spears, D9).

marc-whyte.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: San Antonio City Council District 10, re-elected May 3, 2025 (69%, won outright). Openly Republican on an officially nonpartisan council; Bexar County GOP among endorsers. HARD evidence: opposed ~$170K in public spending on Pride-district rainbow sidewalks (biblical_marriage q4 TRUE — note: argued on fiscal/core-infrastructure grounds, not moral); strong fiscal-restraint record — 'This is a spending problem,' championed zero-based budgeting, called property-tax hikes 'an absolute non-starter' (economic_stewardship TRUE); affirmatively advocated for more police, calling a backtrack on 65 new officers 'government malpractice' and 'add the officers first' (public_justice q0 TRUE); sole member pushing to close the city Migrant Resource Center, opposing city tax dollars for migrant operations (border q2 TRUE — fiscal/safety framing). Supported allowing licensed handgun carry into City Hall, but framed partly as legal compliance with the state AG and as LICENSED (not permitless) carry, so self_defense q0 left null. NO EVIDENCE found on life-at-conception, biblical-marriage affirmation, family/curriculum, Christian liberty, election integrity, or state-overreach/subsidiarity (he generally complied with state directives rather than asserting local control against them) — those cells null, not penalized. Adverse items on record: Dec 2023 DWI (deferred adjudication) and a 2025 ethics-board sanction. Reportedly weighing a 2026 U.S. House run (not yet filed); still seated in D10.

4.note: Opposed ~$170K in public dollars for Pride-district rainbow sidewalks: 'to use public dollars to paint colored sidewalks instead of allocating that money to core infrastructure needs is not in the best interest of our citizens.' Argued on fiscal grounds.

2.note: Strong fiscal-restraint record: 'I don't believe we have a revenue problem. This is a spending problem'; called a property-tax-rate hike 'an absolute non-starter'; championed zero-based budgeting and backed the deepest budget-cut scenario.

0.note: Affirmative pro-police advocacy (not mere budget votes): called a backtrack on hiring 65 officers 'government malpractice'; 'You add the officers first, then you build the rest of the budget around it.'

2.note: Sole council member pushing to close the city's Migrant Resource Center and opposing use of resident tax dollars for migrant operations: 'we should never consider using our residents' tax dollars to fund such an operation.' Center closed Feb 2025.

profile.religion: Active member, Summit Church (San Antonio) — self-stated on campaign site
profile.background: B.B.A. Accounting, Texas Tech; former accountant and senior paralegal in municipal litigation; Director of Constituent Services for Bexar County Commissioner Grant Moody before election. Won the June 2025 D9 runoff (57%), succeeding John Courage. Republican; the council's most reliable conservative with Marc Whyte.

misty-spears.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: San Antonio City Council District 9, won the June 7, 2025 runoff (57%), sworn in June 18, 2025, succeeding John Courage. Verified Republican (ran for Bexar County District Clerk as a Republican in 2022); faith-active (Summit Church, self-stated). HARD evidence: opposed public funding of Pride-district rainbow sidewalks — 'tax dollars should not fund individual viewpoints' (biblical_marriage q4 TRUE, fiscal framing); endorsed the deepest budget-cut scenario and pledged to 'advocate for lower property taxes by cutting inefficiencies,' among the council's most adamant tax-hike opponents (economic_stewardship TRUE); affirmative pro-police advocacy — endorsed by police/fire/sheriff associations, pushed to raise new-officer hiring from 40 to 65, 'I would like to see the 100 officers in year one' (public_justice q0 TRUE); one of two votes against resolutions to obstruct federal immigration enforcement and against zoning limits on detention facilities, calling obstruction 'legally futile' (border q2 TRUE). NO EVIDENCE found of an explicit life-at-conception, one-man-one-woman marriage, family/curriculum, Christian-liberty, election-integrity, deportation-specific (q1), or 2A statement — those cells null, not penalized (most fall outside city-council jurisdiction). Her immigration stance deferred to federal/state authority rather than asserting conservative local control, so refuse_state_overreach left null.

4.note: Publicly opposed the Pride-district rainbow sidewalk project (with Marc Whyte): 'Protected free speech is what makes America the best country in the world. However, tax dollars should not fund individual viewpoints.'

2.note: One of only two members endorsing 'Scenario 1' (deepest cuts, $45M FY26/$128M FY27); campaign pledge to 'advocate for lower property taxes by cutting inefficiencies'; among the council's most adamant tax-hike opponents.

0.note: Affirmative pro-police advocacy: endorsed by police/fire/sheriff associations; pushed (with Whyte) to raise new-officer hires from 40 to 65; 'I would like to see the 100 officers in year one'; refused to cut police/fire even while backing deep cuts elsewhere.

2.note: One of two votes against the resolution to explore interventions against federal immigration enforcement and against zoning limits on detention facilities: 'blocking permits... or otherwise trying to obstruct a federal detention facility is not only misguided, it is legally futile.'

profile.background: Tech executive (founding ED of Tech Bloc; roles at Rackspace, USAA); MBA, DePaul. Daughter of former D7 councilman Joe Alderete. First elected D7 in 2023, re-elected May 2025 (~72%). Democrat-aligned on an officially nonpartisan council, but a labor-plus-business coalition figure who is genuinely pro-police.

marina-alderete-gavito.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: San Antonio City Council District 7, re-elected May 3, 2025 (~72%). Democrat-aligned (endorsed by Stonewall Democrats, Latino Victory Fund) but also by the police and firefighter unions. HARD evidence: documented pro-choice and voted for the abortion-travel/Reproductive Justice posture though her NO on the $100K fund add was on fiscal grounds (sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE); affirmatively pro-LGBTQ — supported the city's first Trans+ History Week ('ready to join the March of Us'), defended the Pride-district rainbow crosswalk ('standing firm... on keeping this crosswalk here'), joined Pride flag-raising (biblical_marriage q2 and q4 FALSE); opposes ICE enforcement — called for a system to track ICE 'civil rights violations' and opposed an ICE detention warehouse (border q1 and q2 FALSE). Genuine pro-police credit: endorsed by SAPOA, co-filed a memo to hire 65 new SAPD officers, 'public safety staffing naturally has to grow' (public_justice q0 TRUE). economic_stewardship left null — anti-property-tax-hike rhetoric but voted for ~$489M arena subsidy, no balanced-budget stance. NO EVIDENCE on marriage-specific affirmation, family/curriculum, Christian liberty, election integrity, subsidiarity, or 2A — null, not penalized.

0.note: Documented pro-choice; her NO on adding $100K to the Reproductive Justice (abortion-travel) Fund was stated on fiscal/core-services grounds, not a personhood position.

2.note: Supported the city's first-ever Trans+ History Week proclamation; stated she is 'ready to join the March of Us event in 2027.'

4.note: Affirmatively promoted LGBTQ identity in public policy: defended the Pride-district rainbow crosswalk ('standing firm with this community'), joined the Pride flag-raising, backed Trans+ History Week.

0.note: Affirmative pro-police: endorsed by the San Antonio Police Officers Association; co-filed a memo pushing to schedule a resolution reaffirming the hire of 65 new SAPD officers — 'public safety staffing naturally has to grow.'

1.note: Opposes ICE enforcement: called for the city to track ICE 'civil rights violations' and opposed an ICE detention warehouse, citing sweeps that 'pick up not just so-called criminals, but women and even infants.'

2.note: Called for a centralized city system to document ICE civil-rights violations and to keep people 'informed of their rights' in ICE encounters — opposite of supporting federal preemption against sanctuary policy.

profile.background: San Antonio City Council District 3 since June 2021, re-elected May 2025 (57%); serving her final allowable term under the city's 8-year cap (term runs to ~2029). Sister Rebecca Viagran previously held D3. Progressive-leaning Democrat on an officially nonpartisan council.

phyllis-viagran.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: San Antonio City Council District 3 since June 2021, re-elected May 2025 (57%), serving her final allowable term. Progressive-leaning Democrat. HARD evidence: voted YES (April 3, 2025, 6-5) to direct ~$100K to fund abortion travel/navigation and co-signed the January 2025 Reproductive Justice Fund memo (sanctity_of_life q0 and q1 FALSE); affirmatively pro-LGBTQ — spoke in support of the first Trans+ History Week and signed a push to expand the LGBTQ non-discrimination ordinance to private businesses (biblical_marriage q2 and q4 FALSE); opposed an ICE detention facility ('I do not support the establishment of any immigration detention facility within the City of San Antonio') and supports migrant services (border q1 and q2 FALSE). public_justice q0 left null — she is not a defunder but does NOT affirmatively champion police (questioned the need for a full 65 new officers, favored technology, co-signed a memo to keep police staffing in the regular budget process rather than a standalone hiring vote); does not meet the affirmative-advocacy bar. economic_stewardship null — opposed a property-tax-RATE increase but backed new fees and voted for the ~$4B budget. self_defense null — gun-safety/secure-storage orientation (lock-box program) but no support for permitless carry and no documented support for red-flag/bans. NO EVIDENCE on family/curriculum, Christian liberty, election integrity, or subsidiarity — null, not penalized.

0.note: Voted YES (6-5, April 3, 2025) to direct ~$100K of city money to fund abortion travel/navigation services; signed the Jan 2025 Reproductive Justice Fund memo — affirmatively pro-abortion-access, contrary to affirming personhood.

1.note: Co-signed the memo to add $100K to the Reproductive Justice Fund and backed abortion-rights resolutions — the opposite of abortion-abolition advocacy.

2.note: Spoke in support of the council's first-ever Trans+ History Week proclamation (May 2026).

4.note: Signed a push to expand the LGBTQ non-discrimination ordinance to private businesses and backed Trans+ History Week — affirmative promotion of LGBTQ identity in public policy.

1.note: 'I do not support the establishment of any immigration detention facility within the City of San Antonio'; supports city migrant-support services.

2.note: Opposed an ICE detention facility and frames the city as not obligated to absorb federal immigration enforcement — opposite of supporting federal preemption against sanctuary policy.

profile.background: San Antonio's youngest council member; B.A. UT Austin; longtime grassroots/voter-mobilization organizer and former Director of Special Projects in the D5 council office. Won the June 7, 2025 D6 runoff by 25 votes; sworn in June 18, 2025. Progressive Democrat on an officially nonpartisan council (endorsed by DSA, AFL-CIO, teachers' unions; Texas Rising/Texas Freedom Network alum). Thin but consistently left-leaning record.

ric-galvan.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: San Antonio City Council District 6, won the June 7, 2025 runoff by 25 votes, sworn in June 18, 2025; currently seated. Progressive Democrat (DSA, AFL-CIO, teachers'-union endorsed; Texas Rising/Texas Freedom Network alum). HARD evidence: publicly supported the city's first Trans+ History Week — 'I have people in my household, I have family... who are trans... I always want to make sure that we are standing firm with them' (biblical_marriage q2 and q4 FALSE); a voting-rights activist tied to Common Cause/'Let Texas Vote,' which opposes strict voter-ID laws (election_integrity q1 FALSE); co-sponsored the Feb 2026 immigration resolution to investigate a moratorium on ICE detention facilities, fund know-your-rights trainings, and explore legal defense, and observed ICE operations to support immigrants (border q1 and q2 FALSE). public_justice q0 left null — equivocal: backed maintaining police funding and distanced himself from the Act4SA police-reform endorsement, but no clear affirmative pro-police advocacy. economic_stewardship null — targets corporate subsidies/tax breaks (restraint) but backs higher social/housing spending. family_child_sovereignty null — public-school/union posture but no explicit anti-school-choice statement. NO EVIDENCE on life, Christian liberty, subsidiarity, or 2A — null, not penalized; institutional progressive ties were NOT used to infer personal positions.

2.note: Spoke for the Trans+ History Week proclamation: 'This is a personal thing for me. I have people in my household... who are trans... I always want to make sure that we are standing firm with them.'

4.note: Publicly affirmed and promoted the city's Trans+ History Week proclamation.

1.note: Longtime voting-rights activist profiled by 'Let Texas Vote' (a Common Cause Texas project that opposes strict voter-ID laws); organized GOTV with Texas Rising — opposite of supporting photo voter ID with citizenship verification.

Co-sponsored the Feb 2026 resolution to support immigrants impacted by federal enforcement — investigate a moratorium on detention facilities, fund know-your-rights trainings, explore legal-defense funding; observed ICE operations to support immigrants.

2.note: Pro-immigrant, anti-detention posture (one of four memo filers); the opposite of opposing sanctuary policy / supporting federal preemption.
